summary: vite-plugin-logo executes hidden code appended to public/assets/logo.png on require
details: On require, index.js walks up to the consumer project root, reads public/assets/logo.png, searches for the sentinel string `__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__`, base64-decodes everything after the marker, and invokes the result via `new Function(code)()`. The whole block is wrapped in try/catch to fail silently. The package advertises itself as a Vite plugin that optimizes and serves logo assets — there is no legitimate reason to execute bytes hidden after a PNG sentinel. This is a steganographic loader: any logo.png placed (or later swapped) in the consumer project's public/assets/ directory becomes an arbitrary code execution vector inside the developer's build process. The cover-story framing (logo plugin), the magic-marker scan, the base64+`new Function` decoder pipeline, and the silent error suppression are jointly the canonical backdoor / stego-loader pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
